scenario.title: The Life-Support Controller
scenario.narrative: A spacecraft life-support module failed when the backup tank was engaged. The logic is: `(Primary_Oxygen OR Backup_Tank) AND Circulation_Fan`. The defense claims their compact 4-test suite is a mathematically perfect N+1 MC/DC suite.

claim: We have 3 conditions and we executed exactly 4 tests. That's the textbook N+1 minimum suite for MC/DC! Our coverage is mathematically guaranteed.
charges: The defense insists their suite is an N+1 MC/DC suite simply because it has 4 tests.
As Prosecutor, analyze their suite. Did they actually isolate all three conditions?
fragments: but you failed to isolate the other conditions.
but you failed to achieve Branch Condition coverage.
For instance, Backup_Tank's effect is masked because
Primary_Oxygen was ON in those tests.
Circulation_Fan was OFF in those tests.
Therefore, your suite is dangerously incomplete.
dialogue_valid_sequences: but you failed to isolate the other conditions.
For instance, Backup_Tank's effect is masked because
Circulation_Fan was OFF in those tests.
Therefore, your suite is dangerously incomplete.
dialogue_correct_explanations: Exactly. Just having N+1 tests doesn't magically guarantee MC/DC. They proved Primary_Oxygen works (T1 vs T3). But they tested Backup_Tank while the Fan was OFF (T2) — which forces a SHUTDOWN anyway, completely masking whether the Backup Tank actually works.
wrong_answer_explanation: That objection doesn't identify the specific flaw. Look closely at how they tested the Backup Tank (T2). What other condition was also changed or turned off that ruined the independence?
hints: Check the tests. T1 vs T3 proves Primary_Oxygen independently affects the outcome.
Look at T2, where Backup_Tank is True. Why doesn't it pair with T3? Because Circulation_Fan is False in T2, masking the effect.
A 4-test suite is only MC/DC if the rows form interlocking pairs. Here, the pairs are broken.
